paper_title: A Verifiable Search Is Not a Learnable Chain-of-Thought
author: Harsh Patel

research_equation.original_formulation: Does a verifiable solver's procedure transfer into a small model's chain-of-thought via SFT? (RQ1: solver coverage → predict learnability; RQ2: binding bottleneck when procedure resists; RQ3: cheap pre-training test)
research_equation.reconstructed_equation: ForwardDerivability(task) ∧ FaithfulCoTExists(task) → SFT_transfers(task). When procedure's only solution = backtracking_search ∧ hidden_structure = information_free → ¬FaithfulCoTExists → SFT learns only verdict_shape → collapse_at_free_running
research_equation.critical_insight: Solver coverage is necessary but badly insufficient. The conditioning variable is not solver accuracy but whether the solver's procedure admits a faithful left-to-right derivation. The causal lever is forward-derivability, isolated by the cipher-key intervention.

pass_1_overview.what_i_read: A controlled study on a benchmark of 9 deterministic-generator tasks, testing whether perfect symbolic solvers can be distilled into a rank-32 LoRA on Nemotron-30B via synthetic CoT SFT.
pass_1_overview.headline: Five of nine tasks transfer readily (≥0.99 for lookup, 0.68 for bit-boolean). One task — cryptarithm — resists everything: 11 CoT designs, RLVR, STaR, all ≤0.07. The binding bottleneck is not capacity, architecture, or training method — it's that the search admits no faithful forward CoT to imitate.
pass_1_overview.surprise_level: High. The author's thoroughness is remarkable: 14 cryptarithm training rounds, 4 backbones, causal intervention, fidelity audit, independent corroboration from two other teams. The verdict-as-token mechanism is crisp and measurable.

key_measurements: Arithmetic correctness 97-100% per line, verdict correctness 16-57%
hit@8 = 0.71 (ranking works), but forward closure = 1/659 (derivation fails)
All 4 backbones hit same ≤0.04 floor on cryptarithm-deduce
pass_2_evidence.robustness_assessment: Extremely high. Convergent evidence from: (1) 11 CoT designs, (2) RLVR/GRPO, (3) STaR, (4) architecture control, (5) causal intervention, (6) per-line fidelity audit, (7) independent 3rd-party at same ceiling, (8) 1st-place solution confirming the escape

limitations.depth_not_breadth: Deep on one benchmark, not broad across many
limitations.single_seeds: One training seed per cell; binomial CIs bound sampling noise but not seed variance
limitations.greedy_eval: Greedy decoding (grader requirement); sampling-based test-time search would change numbers
limitations.missing_cell: Exact 30B/3.5B Transformer match fine-tuned at length not schedulable
limitations.competition_not_won: Best public 0.85, best private 0.86; leader at 0.92

pass_3_synthesis.unifying_narrative: Forward-computable tasks → SFT copies a faithful derivation → works. Backtracking search over information-free structure → no faithful forward derivation exists → SFT copies only the surface form of a verdict (decoupled from its evidence) → collapses at free-running inference. The collapse is a sharp, mechanistic instance of exposure bias localized to the verdict token. Two escapes: (a) teach a search the model can actually run (STaR on self-traces), or (b) precompute the combinatorial core into a memorized catalog and leave only bounded verification in the trace.
pass_3_synthesis.broader_implications: Challenges the widespread assumption that if you can solve a task algorithmically, you can distill the solver into a model. The paper pinpoints why: imitation learning of search procedures fails because autoregressive models cannot maintain carried search state across the generated trace. The finding is architecture-general and scale-invariant (3B to 671B). Practical implication: benchmark designers should measure forward-derivability, not just solver coverage, when predicting whether a task will be CoT-learnable.

challenge_module_map.challenge: Forward-computable procedures do not transfer into CoT
challenge_module_map.resolution: Actually they do — four lookup/arithmetic tasks transfer at ≥0.99. Bit-manipulation transfers at 0.68, with the ceiling being three-tap rules.

challenge_module_map.challenge: Backtracking search (cryptarithm) does not transfer into CoT
challenge_module_map.resolution: This is the paper's main finding. Root cause: no faithful forward CoT exists. Solution via verdict-as-token mechanism. Escape via memorization + verification.
Isolated via causal intervention (cipher key reveal). Forward-derivability is the causal lever, not capacity, architecture, or training method.

challenge_module_map.challenge: Distinguishing between ranking and derivation as the constraint
challenge_module_map.resolution: Generate-and-verify reframe shows hit@8=0.71 (ranking works), forward closure=1/659 (derivation doesn't). Derivation is the bottleneck.

challenge_module_map.challenge: Explaining why SFT loss converges but accuracy stays at floor
challenge_module_map.resolution: Verdict-as-token mechanism: SFT learns the unconditional verdict template (easy) rather than the evidence-conditioned logic (hard). Train NLL drops to floor while held-out accuracy never leaves 0.01-0.07.

challenge_module_map.challenge: Designing the escape from the distillation ceiling
challenge_module_map.resolution: Not the paper's contribution, but documented via 1st-place solution. Remove search from trace: precompute combinatorial core into memorized catalog, leave only recall + bounded verification in CoT.
